items.title: Christine Lagarde, Boris Vujčić: Monetary policy statement
US PPI Climbs at Fastest Pace in More Than Three Years
items.summary: US producer prices increased 6.5% in May from a year earlier, the fastest pace since November 2022, and advanced 1.1% from April as the fallout from the Iran war continued to fan inflation pressures. Michael McKee breaks down the data on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

ECB Hikes for First Time Since 2023 as Inflation Heats Up
items.summary: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in almost three years, concluding it can no longer wait out the Iran war as inflation pressures intensify.

items.summary: The boss of Greece’s Atlantic SEE LNG Trade said it’s becoming more difficult to secure long-term liquefied natural gas deals with US suppliers, after the Iran war upended the global market.

items.title: MFS Collapse Triggers Probe Into Failed UK Lender’s Auditors

items.title:
Opposing militarism is the common will of the international community. 

Japan claims to be a pacifist and defense-oriented country, but its action says otherwise—rocketing military spending, export of lethal weapons, growing offensive capability, revision of its pacifist Constitution, and call for Japan to be a war-capable nation. The list goes on.

Japan has dropped the “pacifist country” mask. The international community must stay on high alert and never allow Japanese militarism to return and again menace the world.

items.title: War cries in the Pacific - Historic premiere for Taiwan HIMARS - Alarm in Beijing over the checkmate move

Deep condolences over the passing of Mr. Yohei Kono, a dear old friend of the Chinese people known for his dedication to China-Japan friendship and commitment to historical justice. Our thoughts go out to his family.

In 1993, Mr. Kono, as Japan’s Chief Cabinet Secretary, officially acknowledged Japan’s responsibility over “comfort women” and expressed remorse and apology in the famous Kono Statement. Regarded as a flagbearer in upholding Japan’s pacifist Constitution, he once said, “Article 9 of the Japanese Constitution, which clearly stipulates that Japan renounces war and does not maintain war potential, represents both our determination, our resolve, and our ideal.”

On his deathbed, he still had China-Japan ties at heart and said in tears that he wished to make one last visit to China. Unfortunately, due to his worsening condition, Mr. Kono’s wish was not fulfilled.

We will always remember Mr. Kono, whose words are particularly relevant today: “History should not be forgotten, the conviction should be carried forward, and the commitments made should always be honored.”

Takaichi Says 100% of Japan’s Oil Supply to Avoid Hormuz in July
items.summary: Japan’s crude oil imports in July are expected to be fully sourced from areas that don’t require transit through the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said Thursday, as she seeks to diversify the resource-poor nation’s energy supply ahead of the hot summer months.

items.title: Has Beijing given up on a nuclear weapon-free Korean peninsula?
items.summary:
China might be downplaying nuclear weapons by not mentioning the issue after Xi Jinping’s visit to North Korea but that does not mean it has accepted its neighbour’s growing arsenal, according to analysts.
